description_long: This is a classic submachine gun with a robust, dark metallic receiver and barrel, complemented by wooden furniture including a thick, contoured stock and ergonomic foregrip. The weapon features a prominent round drum magazine mounted beneath the receiver, suggesting a high ammunition capacity. The barrel is long and cylindrical, with a simple front sight post and a rear sight integrated into the receiver. The wood components have a reddish-brown hue and a visibly grained texture, contrasting with the matte black metal. The overall design is utilitarian, with visible screws and mechanical details, evoking a mid-20th-century military aesthetic. The gun's proportions and features indicate it is designed for two-handed operation, with a focus on stability and sustained fire.
description: A vintage submachine gun with a dark metal body, wooden stock and grips, and a large drum magazine. The design is utilitarian, with visible mechanical details and a mix of wood and metal finishes.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the gun, highlighting the wooden stock, foregrip, and the large drum magazine beneath the receiver.
description_view_1: Front view emphasizing the circular drum magazine and the cylindrical barrel, with the front sight post visible at the muzzle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same wooden and metal features, with the drum magazine and ergonomic grip clearly visible.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the wooden stock and the back of the drum magazine, with the barrel extending forward.
attribute_stock_design: fixed wooden stock with ergonomic contour
attribute_iron_sight_configuration: simple notch rear sight with fixed front post
